# Synthetic Healthcare Dataset — Summary Reference

**Project:** Synaptiq Data Quality Platform  
**Source schema:** [Tuva Project Input Layer](https://thetuvaproject.com/input-layer) (github.com/tuva-health/tuva)  
**Generated by:** `notebooks/generate_tuva_input_layer.py`  
**Catalog:** `dev`  
**Environments:** `prod_main_*` (baseline) and `test_main_*` (drifted)

---

## 1. Catalog Topology

All 30 tables live in the single `dev` catalog. Environment is encoded by schema name prefix.

```
dev
├── prod_main_claims       medical_claim, pharmacy_claim, provider_attribution, location, practitioner
├── prod_main_clinical     encounter, condition, procedure, lab_result, observation,
│                          medication, immunization, appointment, location, practitioner
├── prod_main_members      eligibility, patient, location, practitioner
│
├── test_main_claims       (same tables — drifted population)
├── test_main_clinical     (same tables — drifted population)
└── test_main_members      (same tables — drifted population)
```

`location` and `practitioner` are written identically to all six schemas using a fixed seed — they act as **negative-control tables** (expected PSI ≈ 0 when profiled side-by-side).

---

## 2. Table Schemas

### 2.1 medical_claim — 58 columns
_One row per claim line. Primary key: `claim_id`, `claim_line_number`, `data_source`._

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| claim_id | varchar | Groups lines within same claim |
| claim_line_number | integer | 1-indexed within claim_id |
| claim_type | varchar | `professional`, `institutional`, `undetermined` |
| person_id | varchar | Links to eligibility, patient |
| member_id | varchar | Payer-assigned coverage ID |
| payer | varchar | `Aetna` |
| plan | varchar | `Aetna PPO` |
| claim_start_date | date | Header-level |
| claim_end_date | date | Header-level |
| claim_line_start_date | date | Line-level |
| claim_line_end_date | date | Line-level |
| admission_date | date | Inpatient only |
| discharge_date | date | Inpatient only |
| admit_source_code | varchar | Inpatient only |
| admit_type_code | varchar | Inpatient only |
| discharge_disposition_code | varchar | Inpatient only |
| place_of_service_code | varchar | 2-digit CMS POS code |
| bill_type_code | varchar | Institutional only |
| drg_code_type | varchar | `ms-drg` / `apr-drg` |
| drg_code | varchar | Inpatient only |
| revenue_center_code | varchar | Institutional only |
| service_unit_quantity | integer | Units rendered |
| hcpcs_code | varchar | CPT / HCPCS code |
| hcpcs_modifier_1..5 | varchar | Modifiers (mostly null) |
| rendering_npi | varchar | Provider who rendered service |
| rendering_tin | varchar | Rendering provider TIN |
| billing_npi | varchar | Billing provider NPI |
| billing_tin | varchar | Billing provider TIN |
| facility_npi | varchar | Facility NPI (institutional only) |
| paid_date | date | Null when denied/pending |
| paid_amount | float | 0 when denied |
| allowed_amount | float | Null when denied |
| charge_amount | float | **Primary drift signal** |
| coinsurance_amount | float | Null when denied |
| copayment_amount | float | Null when denied |
| deductible_amount | float | ~30% populated |
| total_cost_amount | float | = allowed_amount |
| diagnosis_code_type | varchar | `icd-10-cm` |
| diagnosis_code_1 | varchar | Primary diagnosis — always populated |
| diagnosis_code_2..15 | varchar | Additional diagnoses — mostly null |
| data_source | varchar | `SynaptiqPOC` |

---

### 2.2 eligibility — 47 columns
_One enrollment span per person. Primary key: `person_id`, `data_source`._

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| person_id | varchar | Links to all clinical/claims tables |
| member_id | varchar | |
| subscriber_id | varchar | |
| subscriber_relation | varchar | `self` for all synthetic records |
| enrollment_start_date | date | ~10% have mid-year start |
| enrollment_end_date | date | PROD: 2024-12-31 / TEST: 2025-03-31 |
| payer | varchar | `Aetna` |
| payer_type | varchar | 60% commercial, 20% medicaid, 20% medicare |
| plan | varchar | `Aetna PPO` |
| first_name / last_name | varchar | Synthetic (`First000001` style) |
| gender | varchar | 48% male, 52% female |
| race | varchar | Per US population distribution |
| birth_date | date | Ages 18–85 |
| death_date / death_flag | date / bool | All null / 0 in synthetic data |
| dual_status_code | varchar | Null (not modeled) |
| medicare_status_code | varchar | Null (not modeled) |
| metal_level | varchar | Null (not modeled) |
| group_id / group_name | varchar | 200 employer groups |
| data_source | varchar | `SynaptiqPOC` |

_(Remaining ~25 Medicare/ACA-specific columns are all null in synthetic data — realistic for a commercial payer POC.)_

---

### 2.3 pharmacy_claim — 26 columns
_One row per pharmacy claim line. Primary key: `claim_id`, `claim_line_number`, `data_source`._

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| claim_id | varchar | |
| claim_line_number | integer | Always 1 in synthetic data |
| person_id | varchar | |
| member_id | varchar | |
| prescribing_provider_npi | varchar | From practitioner pool |
| dispensing_provider_npi | varchar | From practitioner pool |
| dispensing_date | date | |
| ndc_code | varchar | 10 common drugs (see §4) |
| quantity | integer | |
| days_supply | integer | 30 or 90 days |
| refills | integer | 0–5 |
| paid_amount | float | |
| allowed_amount | float | |
| charge_amount | float | |
| coinsurance_amount | float | |
| copayment_amount | float | |
| deductible_amount | float | ~30% populated |
| in_network_flag | boolean | |
| data_source | varchar | `SynaptiqPOC` |

---

### 2.4 patient — 24 columns
_One row per person. Same data both environments (reference). Primary key: `person_id`, `data_source`._

Columns: `person_id`, `patient_id`, `first_name`, `middle_name`, `last_name`, `name_suffix`, `sex`, `race`, `ethnicity`, `birth_date`, `death_date`, `death_flag`, `social_security_number`, `address`, `city`, `state`, `zip_code`, `county`, `latitude`, `longitude`, `phone`, `email`, `ingest_datetime`, `data_source`

---

### 2.5 encounter — 22 columns
_One row per clinical encounter. Primary key: `encounter_id`, `data_source`._

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| encounter_id | varchar | `ENC-PROD-000001` / `ENC-TEST-000001` |
| person_id | varchar | |
| encounter_type | varchar | See §4 for distribution |
| encounter_start_date / end_date | date | Duration varies by type |
| admit_source_code | varchar | Inpatient only |
| discharge_disposition_code | varchar | Inpatient only |
| attending_provider_id | varchar | |
| facility_npi | varchar | |
| primary_diagnosis_code | varchar | ICD-10-CM |
| drg_code | varchar | Inpatient only |
| paid_amount / allowed_amount / charge_amount | float | |
| data_source | varchar | |

---

### 2.6 condition — 16 columns
_2–4 conditions per encounter. Primary key: `condition_id`, `data_source`._

Columns: `condition_id`, `person_id`, `patient_id`, `encounter_id`, `recorded_date`, `onset_date`, `resolved_date`, `status`, `condition_type`, `source_code_type` (`icd-10-cm`), `source_code`, `source_description`, `condition_rank`, `present_on_admit_code`, `ingest_datetime`, `data_source`

Status distribution: 60% active, 30% resolved, 10% historical.

---

### 2.7 procedure — 16 columns
_1–3 procedures per encounter. Primary key: `procedure_id`, `data_source`._

Columns: `procedure_id`, `person_id`, `patient_id`, `encounter_id`, `procedure_date`, `source_code_type` (`CPT`), `source_code`, `source_description`, `modifier_1..5`, `practitioner_id`, `ingest_datetime`, `data_source`

---

### 2.8 lab_result — 27 columns
_1–3 lab results per encounter. Primary key: `lab_result_id`, `data_source`._

Key columns: `lab_result_id`, `person_id`, `encounter_id`, `source_order_code` (LOINC), `source_component_code` (LOINC), `result` (string), `result_datetime`, `collection_datetime`, `source_units`, `source_reference_range_low/high`, `source_abnormal_flag`, `specimen`, `ordering_practitioner_id`

**HbA1c (LOINC 4548-4) is the primary drift signal** — see §5.

---

### 2.9 observation — 19 columns
_2–4 vital signs per encounter. Primary key: `observation_id`, `data_source`._

Key columns: `observation_id`, `person_id`, `encounter_id`, `panel_id`, `observation_date`, `observation_type` (`vital-signs`), `source_code` (LOINC), `result` (string), `source_units`, reference range fields

**BMI (LOINC 39156-5) is a drift signal** — see §5.

---

### 2.10 medication — 20 columns
_0–2 medications per encounter. Primary key: `medication_id`, `data_source`._

Columns: `medication_id`, `person_id`, `encounter_id`, `dispensing_date`, `prescribing_date`, `source_code_type`, `source_code`, `ndc_code`, `rxnorm_code`, `atc_code`, `route`, `strength`, `quantity`, `quantity_unit`, `days_supply`, `practitioner_id`, `ingest_datetime`, `data_source`

---

### 2.11 immunization — 18 columns
_~3 per person. Primary key: `immunization_id`, `data_source`._

Columns: `immunization_id`, `person_id`, `patient_id`, `encounter_id`, `source_code_type` (`CVX`), `source_code`, `source_description`, `status`, `status_reason`, `occurrence_date`, `source_dose`, `lot_number`, `body_site`, `route`, `location_id`, `practitioner_id`, `ingest_datetime`, `data_source`

---

### 2.12 appointment — 16 columns
_~5 per person. Primary key: `appointment_id`, `data_source`._

Columns: `appointment_id`, `person_id`, `patient_id`, `encounter_id`, `start_datetime`, `end_datetime`, `duration`, `location_id`, `practitioner_id`, `type_code`, `type_description`, `status_code`, `status_description`, `reason`, `cancellation_reason`, `data_source`

Status distribution: 70% completed, 15% canceled, 10% no-show, 5% scheduled.

---

### 2.13 practitioner — 8 columns
_200 records. Static — identical both environments._

`practitioner_id`, `npi`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `practice_affiliation`, `specialty`, `sub_specialty`, `data_source`

10 specialties: Family Medicine, Internal Medicine, Cardiology, Orthopedic Surgery, Emergency Medicine, Psychiatry, Radiology, Gastroenterology, Neurology, Endocrinology.

---

### 2.14 location — 12 columns
_50 records. Static — identical both environments._

`location_id`, `npi`, `name`, `facility_type`, `parent_organization`, `address`, `city`, `state`, `zip_code`, `latitude`, `longitude`, `data_source`

6 facility types: hospital, clinic, emergency department, ambulatory surgery center, laboratory, imaging center.

---

### 2.15 provider_attribution — 16 columns
_One record per person per year. Primary key: `person_id`, `year_month`, `data_source`._

`person_id`, `member_id`, `year_month`, `payer`, `plan`, `payer_attributed_provider`, `payer_attributed_provider_practice`, `payer_attributed_provider_organization`, `payer_attributed_provider_lob`, `custom_attributed_provider`, `custom_attributed_provider_practice`, `custom_attributed_provider_organization`, `custom_attributed_provider_lob`, `file_name`, `ingest_datetime`, `data_source`

---

## 3. Population and Row Counts

### Shared population (same person_ids used in all tables)

| Entity | Count |
|---|---|
| Unique persons | 5,000 |
| Practitioners | 200 |
| Locations | 50 |
| Employer groups | 200 |

### Row counts per environment (PROD and TEST separately)

| Table | Expected rows | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| medical_claim | ~50,000 | 1–3 lines per encounter |
| pharmacy_claim | ~3,000 | Independent of encounters |
| provider_attribution | ~5,000 | One per person |
| encounter | 25,000 | Fixed per side |
| condition | ~75,000 | 2–4 per encounter |
| procedure | ~50,000 | 1–3 per encounter |
| lab_result | ~50,000 | 1–3 per encounter |
| observation | ~75,000 | 2–4 per encounter (vitals) |
| medication | ~20,000 | 0–2 per encounter |
| immunization | ~15,000 | ~3 per person |
| appointment | ~25,000 | ~5 per person |
| eligibility | 5,000 | One span per person |
| patient | 5,000 | Static — same both sides |
| practitioner | 200 | Static — same both sides |
| location | 50 | Static — same both sides |
| **Total per side** | **~408,250** | |
| **Grand total (both sides)** | **~816,500** | |

### Date ranges

| Environment | Date range |
|---|---|
| PROD | 2024-01-01 → 2024-12-31 |
| TEST | 2024-07-01 → 2025-03-31 |

---

## 5. Drift Characteristics (PROD vs TEST)

### 5.1 charge_amount — medical_claim (Primary drift signal)

| Metric | PROD | TEST |
|---|---|---|
| Distribution | Lognormal(μ=6.9, σ=0.80) | Lognormal(μ=7.2, σ=0.85) |
| Expected mean | ~$1,400 | ~$1,900 |
| Expected median | ~$990 | ~$1,330 |
| Expected PSI | — | **~0.13 (moderate)** |
| Expected verdict | — | `moderate` |

The 35% increase in the lognormal mean shifts the entire right tail of the distribution, producing consistent moderate drift detectable by PSI, KS, and JS divergence.

### 5.2 Denial rate — medical_claim

| Metric | PROD | TEST |
|---|---|---|
| Denial rate | 15% | 20% |
| Effect on paid_amount | 15% of rows = 0 | 20% of rows = 0 |
| Effect on allowed_amount | 15% null | 20% null |
| Effect on paid_date | 15% null | 20% null |
| Expected PSI (denial_code column) | — | **stable (~0.05)** |

The denial rate shift is intentionally subtle — enough to change null patterns but not large enough to trigger a significant PSI verdict on the denial_code column alone.

### 5.3 claim_type distribution — medical_claim

| claim_type | PROD | TEST | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| professional | 60% | 55% | −5 pp |
| institutional | ~25% | ~28% | +3 pp |
| undetermined | ~15% | ~17% | +2 pp |
| Expected PSI | — | **stable (<0.05)** | |

### 5.4 HbA1c — lab_result (LOINC 4548-4)

| Metric | PROD | TEST |
|---|---|---|
| Distribution | Normal(μ=7.2, σ=1.5), clipped [4.5, 13.0] | Normal(μ=8.1, σ=1.5), clipped [4.5, 13.0] |
| Expected mean | 7.2 | 8.1 |
| Interpretation | Controlled diabetic population | Higher-acuity / less-controlled TEST population |
| Expected PSI | — | **~0.10–0.15 (moderate)** |

### 5.5 BMI — observation (LOINC 39156-5)

| Metric | PROD | TEST |
|---|---|---|
| Distribution | Normal(μ=27.5, σ=5.0), clipped [15, 55] | Normal(μ=29.2, σ=5.0), clipped [15, 55] |
| Expected mean | 27.5 (overweight threshold) | 29.2 (approaching obese threshold) |
| Expected PSI | — | **stable to moderate (~0.08)** |

### 5.6 Negative control tables (expected zero drift)

These tables are generated with the same fixed seed for both environments and should show PSI ≈ 0 when profiled. Use them to verify the profiler does not false-positive on stable data.

| Table | Expected PSI | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| location | ~0.00 | Identical 50-record dataset |
| practitioner | ~0.00 | Identical 200-record dataset |
| patient | ~0.00 | Identical 5,000-record dataset |

### 5.7 Summary drift matrix

| Table | Column | Expected verdict | Signal type |
|---|---|---|---|
| medical_claim | charge_amount | **moderate** | Distribution shift |
| medical_claim | paid_amount | moderate | Downstream of denial rate |
| medical_claim | allowed_amount | stable–moderate | Downstream of denial rate |
| medical_claim | paid_date (null %) | stable | Null rate shift |
| medical_claim | claim_type | stable | Small mix shift |
| lab_result | result (HbA1c) | **moderate** | Mean shift |
| observation | result (BMI) | stable–moderate | Mean shift |
| encounter | charge_amount | moderate | Same lognormal shift |
| eligibility | enrollment_end_date | stable | Date range shift only |
| location | all columns | **stable** | Negative control |
| practitioner | all columns | **stable** | Negative control |
| patient | all columns | **stable** | Negative control |

---

## 6. Recommended Profiler Run Pairs

| Profile comparison | Side A | Side B | Expected finding |
|---|---|---|---|
| Claims financials | `dev.prod_main_claims.medical_claim` | `dev.test_main_claims.medical_claim` | Moderate drift on charge_amount, paid_amount |
| Lab values | `dev.prod_main_clinical.lab_result` | `dev.test_main_clinical.lab_result` | Moderate drift on HbA1c result |
| Vitals | `dev.prod_main_clinical.observation` | `dev.test_main_clinical.observation` | Stable–moderate drift on BMI |
| Negative control | `dev.prod_main_claims.location` | `dev.test_main_claims.location` | All stable — no drift |
| Member enrollment | `dev.prod_main_members.eligibility` | `dev.test_main_members.eligibility` | Stable — date range shift only |

---

## 7. Reproducibility

All generators use `numpy.random.default_rng(seed)` with fixed seeds:

| Environment | Seed |
|---|---|
| PROD | 42 |
| TEST | 99 |
| Static reference tables | 0, 1, 2 (location, practitioner, patient) |

Re-running the notebook produces bit-for-bit identical tables. To generate a new random population, change `SEED_PROD` and `SEED_TEST` at the top of the notebook.
